questions.text: When you face a tough problem, what do you usually do?

options.label: Break it down step by step — collect facts and study them carefully
Get hands-on — try things out until something works
Talk it through — ask people and hear different views
Think of a completely different idea that no one has tried

questions.text: When you don't know how to do something new, you prefer to...

options.label: Read about it and look it up until I feel confident
Just try it out and learn as I go
Ask someone who already knows how
Watch someone else do it first, then copy and improve

questions.text: What kind of work makes you feel most proud?

options.label: I solved a hard problem that no one else could figure out
I built or fixed something real that you can see and touch
I helped someone through a tough situation
I made something original that didn't exist before

questions.text: What frustrates you most at work or school?

options.label: Messy, unclear tasks — I never know what is expected
Boring, repeated tasks — doing the same thing again and again
Working alone for too long with no one to talk to
Strict rules that stop me from being creative

questions.text: How do you like to learn best?

options.label: Reading and thinking about ideas on my own
Doing experiments or physical activities
Discussing and debating with others
Drawing, building models, or making things

questions.text: When you finish a big project, what matters most to you?

options.label: It was logically correct and done very carefully
It was genuinely useful to the people who needed it
It looked or sounded great — it was well-made
You built it yourself — with your own hands and effort

questions.text: Which work place excites you most?

options.label: A lab or research centre — quiet, step-by-step, focused on discovery
An office or desk — organised, digital, professional
Outdoors — forests, fields, building sites, or local communities
A studio or creative space — screens, sketchbooks, and music

questions.text: Imagine your perfect workday — where are you?

options.label: In a hospital or clinic — with patients and fellow staff
In a tech office — writing code, studying data, or designing products
In a courtroom or government office — law, policy, or running the government
In a classroom or community space — teaching and working with people

questions.text: Which setting would feel most satisfying to you?

options.label: A workshop — building, fixing, and making things
A stage or studio — performing, recording, or directing
Nature or the field — forests, farms, rivers, or villages
A library or research space — deep in books and knowledge

questions.text: How do you prefer your work to be judged?

options.label: Clear results — numbers and data that show what I achieved
The trust and good relationships I build with people
The creativity and originality of what I make
Whether I followed the right process and rules correctly

questions.text: Which work speed suits you best?

options.label: Fast and unpredictable — emergencies, quick decisions
Slow and steady — deep projects that go on for months
Creative and varied — bursts of energy and time to think
Organised and predictable — clear routine, fixed hours

questions.text: Where do you feel most energetic and alive?

options.label: Around machines, tools, or systems — working with my hands
Around people in need — patients, students, or communities
In front of an audience — performing, presenting, inspiring
Deep in a research problem — alone with ideas and data

questions.text: How much do you enjoy working directly with people every day?

options.label: I love it — it is the best part of work for me
A mix — sometimes with people, sometimes working alone
I prefer working behind the scenes — with tools, data, or systems
Depends on the task — I do not have a strong preference

questions.text: What kind of work with people do you prefer?

options.label: Helping people in need — patients, those in crisis, or struggling communities
Teaching and guiding others to grow
Convincing and selling — persuading people about ideas or products
Working with teammates on a shared technical goal

questions.text: In a group project, what role suits you naturally?

options.label: The leader — I organise everyone and keep us on track
The idea person — I come up with the creative sparks
The doer — I put my head down and get it done
The communicator — I make sure everyone is on the same page

questions.text: Would you rather your work helps people...

options.label: Directly — as a patient, student, or client you personally serve
Through products, systems, or policies that reach many people
Both — I want to see the personal impact and the big picture
I prefer working with data or ideas more than with people directly

questions.text: How do you feel about speaking in front of a group?

options.label: I love it — being in front of an audience gives me energy
I can do it when needed — it does not bother me
I avoid it if I can — I prefer writing or working behind the scenes
I prefer one-on-one conversations over talking to a crowd

questions.text: Do you prefer work relationships to be...

options.label: Deep and long-term — same people, strong bonds built over years
Varied — meeting new people often, different clients and teams
Mostly professional and task-based
Online or digital — remote work, online tools

questions.text: How do you feel about high-pressure, high-stakes situations?

options.label: I do well under pressure — it brings out the best in me
Okay if it happens sometimes — not every single day
I prefer calm, low-pressure work
I need a balance — challenging but not always stressful

questions.text: Which appeals to you more?

options.label: Saving lives or making decisions that directly affect people
Building products or systems that millions of people use
Teaching and shaping the next generation
Advising businesses or governments on big decisions

questions.text: Which career trade-off would you choose?

options.label: Less pay but work that makes a real difference to society
High pay in a company or professional setting
Freedom to be creative — even if the income is uncertain
A stable government job — security and public service

questions.text: Do you want to be known as a deep expert or someone good at many things?

options.label: A deep expert — the top person in one specific area
Someone good at many things — connecting ideas from different fields
A leader who manages experts
A researcher who discovers things no one has found before

questions.text: If your work has a mistake, which feels most serious to you?

options.label: A patient or client gets hurt directly
A product bug that thousands of users face
A student's learning or confidence gets hurt
The company loses money or its good name

questions.text: In healthcare, what attracts you most?

options.label: Direct patient care — checking, treating, and following up with patients
Surgery and procedures — careful, hands-on operations
Research — understanding diseases and finding new cures
Mental health support — understanding and helping heal the mind

questions.text: Which healthcare situation excites you most?

options.label: Treating a very sick patient who fully recovers
Publishing a research paper that changes how doctors treat a disease
Designing a programme that stops disease from spreading across a whole region
Talking with someone going through a mental health crisis until they feel safe

questions.text: How do you feel about doing the same type of procedure or check-up again and again?

options.label: That is fine — repeating builds skill and I want to be the best at it
I prefer variety — different kinds of patient cases each day
I would prefer research where each problem is different
I prefer the management or admin side of healthcare

questions.text: Medical emergencies — what is your reaction?

options.label: It excites me — I want to be the first one to respond
Important, but I would prefer planned surgeries with more control
I would rather focus on long-term care and prevention
I prefer non-emergency health education and mental health work

questions.text: Which medical career path sounds most meaningful to you?

options.label: Being the doctor patients know by name — a trusted family doctor
A surgeon who performs difficult, life-saving operations
A scientist who discovers new treatments and cures
A public health officer who shapes healthcare policy for millions

questions.text: Which bothers you more?

options.label: A patient suffering needlessly when treatment is available
A disease that has no cure yet — because no one has understood it
Communities that have no access to even basic healthcare
Someone whose mental pain goes ignored and unnoticed

questions.text: How do you feel about years of hard study and tough exams like NEET?

options.label: Ready for it — the goal is worth every bit of the effort
Willing, but I would like to know what other paths exist too
Worried about the pressure — it feels like too much
Not for me — I want health careers that do not need NEET

questions.text: In technology, what appeals most to you?

options.label: Writing code and building apps that people use every day
Looking at data to find patterns and insights no one has spotted
Designing how things look and feel to users
The hardware side — circuits, chips, servers, physical systems

questions.text: Which tech project would excite you most?

options.label: Building a mobile app from scratch — from idea to launch
Training an AI model on health data to predict outcomes
Redesigning a confusing website to be beautiful and easy to use
Setting up a strong, safe network for a hospital

questions.text: When you use an app or website, what do you notice first?

options.label: How the code might be built underneath
What data it collects and what patterns it might show
How good the design and user flow feels
Whether it would handle lots of users and stay secure

questions.text: Which sounds most interesting to you?

options.label: Software that runs on hundreds of millions of phones
A bridge or building that will still be standing in 100 years
A data model that predicts stock prices or disease outbreaks
A network that protects banks and hospitals from hackers

questions.text: In engineering, which area draws you in?

options.label: Software and digital systems — apps, platforms, programs
Electrical and electronics — circuits, sensors, communication systems
Mechanical, civil, or structural — machines, bridges, buildings
Chemical or materials — processes, substances, manufacturing

questions.text: What result from your work would matter most to you?

options.label: Users love using what I built — great experience, high ratings
The maths and logic is correct and elegant
It solved a real physical problem — you can see and touch the result
It keeps private data safe and systems running securely

questions.text: If you had to specialise, which skills would you most enjoy learning deeply?

options.label: Programming languages and step-by-step problem solving
Statistics, machine learning, and data modelling
Visual design, user research, and making test models
Physical systems, design tools, and real-world engineering

questions.text: In business, what gives you energy?

options.label: Building and growing your own company from scratch
Studying companies and markets to make smart investment decisions
Checking if financial records are correct and rules are being followed
Convincing customers to choose a product — marketing and branding

questions.text: What kind of business challenge appeals to you?

options.label: Starting a new business — building something from zero
Figuring out what a company is really worth
Checking a company's accounts — making sure every number is correct
Running a marketing campaign — reaching people and changing their minds

questions.text: Which financial skill would you most want?

options.label: Judging a company's potential — its growth, risk, and future value
Making sure accounts are legally correct and rules are followed
Managing a set of investments across different markets
Using economics to predict how markets and government policies affect each other

questions.text: When you think about money in your career...

options.label: I want to help others manage or grow their money
I want to build something that earns a lot of money
I want to study it and find opportunities in the market
I want to make sure every rupee is counted and recorded correctly

questions.text: Which work culture fits you?

options.label: A fast-moving startup — high risk, high energy, figure it out as you go
A big company with clear processes, defined roles, and professional growth
A consulting firm — different client problems to solve every week
Independent research or analysis — thinking deeply without many meetings

questions.text: What is most exciting about markets and business to you?

options.label: The strategy — beating rivals and winning more customers
The numbers behind it — making decisions using data
The people and relationships — building trust and networks
The creative and branding side — what makes people pick one thing over another

questions.text: In creative work, what form feels most natural to you?

options.label: Visual — images, colour, illustration, graphic design
Written — stories, articles, scripts, essays
Audio — music, singing, sound, voice
Performance — acting, dance, spoken word, movement

questions.text: What kind of creative work do you most want to make?

options.label: A film or documentary people watch and remember
A book, article, or piece of writing people read and share
Music or audio that people listen to and feel something
A design or visual identity that makes things more beautiful and easy to use

questions.text: Who is your audience?

options.label: Students you want to inspire and guide
The general public you want to inform or make curious
A brand's customers you want to reach and influence
Anyone who experiences your art — no particular target

questions.text: Which medium excites you most?

options.label: Camera and video editing — filming and cutting stories together
Stage and live performance — theatre, music, or dance
A blank canvas or sketchpad — illustration, painting, or design
A keyboard and words — writing stories, articles, or scripts

questions.text: In education, what appeals to you?

options.label: Explaining hard ideas simply so anyone can understand
Designing how learning happens — what is taught, tools, and systems
Mentoring students one-on-one and watching them grow
Researching better ways to teach and checking what actually works

questions.text: Which recognition would mean most to you?

options.label: A student saying 'you changed my life'
An audience giving you a standing ovation
A major design or industry award
A bestselling book or a piece of writing that goes viral

questions.text: What kind of impact do you want to have?

options.label: Discover new scientific knowledge that changes how the world understands something
Fight for justice for people who have been wronged
Make policies that improve the lives of millions
Protect the environment and natural world for future generations

questions.text: Which path appeals to you?

options.label: Years in a lab — discovering something that has never been known before
Years in court — arguing cases and upholding the law
Years in civil services — managing districts and running the government
Years doing fieldwork — in communities, in nature, on the ground

questions.text: Which problem would you most want to solve?

options.label: A disease that has no cure yet
An unfair law that harms people in need
A government system that wastes public money and fails citizens
Climate change, pollution, and the destruction of nature

questions.text: How do you feel about authority and power?

options.label: Comfortable — I want to use authority to enforce rules and drive change
I prefer advising and persuading — not giving orders
I would rather work inside the system and change it from within
I prefer independent research — not using official power

questions.text: Which daily work sounds most right for you?

options.label: Running experiments, reading papers, and writing up findings
Preparing legal arguments and representing clients in court
Managing government programmes, teams, and public resources
Visiting communities, collecting data, and running programmes on the ground

questions.text: What drives you more?

options.label: Curiosity — really understanding how the world works
Fairness and justice — making sure people are treated right
Public service — serving the country and its people
Conservation — protecting nature and the environment

questions.text: What matters more to you in a career?

options.label: High income — I want to earn well and build financial security
Making a difference — I want my work to have real impact
Creative freedom — doing work I love in my own way
Stability and security — a reliable career path

questions.text: Where do you want to live and work?

options.label: In a major Indian city — Mumbai, Bangalore, Delhi
Anywhere in India — open to wherever the work takes me
Willing to travel internationally for the right opportunity
My hometown or a smaller city — roots and community matter to me

questions.text: How important is job security to you?

options.label: Very important — I want a safe, guaranteed job
Somewhat important — good prospects but not at any cost
Less important — I will take risk for a bigger reward
Not at all — I will build my own path

questions.text: Which describes your ambition best?

options.label: Become the best in my field — known as a top expert
Build something of my own — a company, a clinic, a brand
Rise to leadership — manage teams, organisations, or institutions
Contribute quietly but meaningfully — do great work without the fame

questions.text: Which work style fits you?

options.label: Deep focus on one complex thing for hours at a time
Switching between different tasks and people throughout the day
Creative and free — exploring ideas without fixed plans
Clear tasks with measurable results — I know exactly what I achieved

questions.text: How do you feel about competition?

options.label: I love it — competing brings out my best
It helps in small amounts — I like a healthy challenge
I prefer working together over competing
I only compete with my past self — personal growth over rankings

questions.text: How do you feel about routine?

options.label: I like structure and consistent patterns — it helps me do my best work
I want every day to be different — variety keeps me going
Some routine is fine but I need space to be creative
I want to design my own routine — not follow someone else's

questions.text: When you think about your future, what matters most?

options.label: Being respected and well known in my field
Financial freedom — having the freedom that money brings
Having a positive impact on society — leaving things better than I found them
Living a balanced, happy life with time for what matters to me

questions.text: How do you feel about paperwork, documentation, and following rules?

options.label: Fine with it — being careful and following process matters
Okay with it if the work itself is meaningful
I prefer to keep it minimum — I want to focus on the real work
I really dislike it — too much paperwork and rules slow everything down

questions.text: Which describes you best?

options.label: I like knowing exactly what is expected of me — clarity helps me work well
I like figuring things out as I go — the path reveals itself
I like setting the direction for others — I am a natural planner and leader
I like mixing structure with creative freedom — the best of both
